abstract: How does the balance between text and pictorial content in marketer-generated social media posts affect user engagement? The authors address this question by using computer vision and natural language processing tools to extract the visual and textual features of 34,610 organic brand posts from Facebook and Instagram. Using a confounding-and-cluster-robust causal forests model, they test how the balance of text and picture affects social media engagement across content and visual contexts. Results show that posts with greater emphasis on overlay text over pictorial content tend to have fewer likes and comments. However, the performance of text-oriented posts improves if text is more centered, informative, emotionally positive, and congruent with the pictorial content and if the picture contains fewer prominent objects or less information such as social cues. They quantify how incremental changes in such content composition affect social media engagement. These findings set forth evidence-based principles for optimizing text and picture balance in marketer-generated content and provide actionable guidelines on whether, where, when, and how to present text on an image. This research highlights the potential for transforming content and media creation from an imprecise art form into an empirical science nested within a data-driven visual optimization framework.

ob.ai_reason: 使用计算机视觉和NLP技术分析内容，但核心是营销组合策略
ob.tldr: 研究营销者在社交媒体发布的文字与图片配比对用户互动的影响。使用计算机视觉和自然语言处理抽取34,610条品牌帖子的多模态特征，采用因果森林模型进行因果推断。结果显示，过多叠加文字会降低点赞和评论；但若文字居中、信息丰富、情感积极且与图片一致，文本导向帖子的表现可提升。该研究提供文字‑图片平衡的实证优化原则和具体操作指南。

ur.tldr: 研究考察营销生成的社交媒体帖子中文字与图片比例对用户互动的影响，运用计算机视觉和自然语言处理提取特征，借助因果森林模型分析 34,610 条帖子。结果表明，文字比重过大降低点赞和评论；但文字居中、信息丰富、情感积极且与图片一致、图片简洁时，文字导向帖子表现更佳，提供内容组合的优化原则。

title: When Words Meet Visuals: How Content Composition Drives Social Media Engagement for Marketer-Generated Content
title_zh: 文字与视觉的交汇：内容组合如何驱动营销生成内容在社交媒体上的互动
